title: Design System: La Transustanciación en los Padres de la Iglesia

display.purpose: Hero title only. One per page. Fraunces italic for the theological word; upright for the structural word.

headline.purpose: Section heads and decorative panel ordinals (at opacity 0.35). Structural framing.

title.purpose: Panel headings, conflict titles, accordion summaries.

body.purpose: Primary reading text. Max 65–75ch line length.

label.purpose: Scholarly apparatus: kickers, debater labels, source citations, metadata. IBM Plex Mono only. Never for paragraph text.

shadows.name: soft
shadows.purpose: Default ambient shadow for cards, tiles, and panels at rest.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: Hover and :target active state. The highest-elevation surface on screen.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for color and opacity transitions.

motion.name: ease-lift
motion.purpose: Card hover lift (translateY). Decelerates quickly — physical, not bouncy.

motion.name: ease-menu
motion.purpose: Mobile nav slide-in and other panel reveals.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Single-column to two-column transitions (tile grids, debate-face pairs).

breakpoints.name: desktop
breakpoints.purpose: Four-column tile grid, full navigation links visible.

components.name: Arg Tile — Urbina
components.description: Clickable tile that activates a detail panel via :target. Catholic/Navy variant.
components.css: .ds-tile-urbina { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 1rem; background: linear-gradient(160deg, #ffffff, #E8EFF8); border: 1px solid #DAD3C6; border-left: 4px solid #1B4F8A; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,24,20,0.04), 0 8px 24px -12px rgba(26,24,20,0.18); text-decoration: none; text-align: center; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-tile-urbina:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 2px 4px rgba(26,24,20,0.06), 0 20px 48px -20px rgba(26,24,20,0.28); border-color: #1B4F8A; } .ds-tile-urbina:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C4922A; outline-offset: 2px; transform: translateY(-3px); } .ds-tile-n { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 1.2rem; line-height: 1; color: #1B4F8A; } .ds-tile-keyword { font-family: 'Spectral', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #163F6E; line-height: 1.2; }

components.name: Arg Tile — Pacheco
components.description: Clickable tile that activates a detail panel via :target. Evangelical/Crimson variant.
components.html: 01Anacronismo
components.css: .ds-tile-pacheco { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 1rem; background: linear-gradient(160deg, #ffffff, #F5EAEA); border: 1px solid #DAD3C6; border-left: 4px solid #7A2E2E; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,24,20,0.04), 0 8px 24px -12px rgba(26,24,20,0.18); text-decoration: none; text-align: center; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-tile-pacheco:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 2px 4px rgba(26,24,20,0.06), 0 20px 48px -20px rgba(26,24,20,0.28); border-color: #7A2E2E; } .ds-tile-pacheco:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C4922A; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-tile-n--pacheco { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 1.2rem; line-height: 1; color: #7A2E2E; } .ds-tile-keyword--pacheco { font-family: 'Spectral', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #5F2222; line-height: 1.2; }

components.name: Prose Card
components.description: Informational card for the Contexto section. White surface on Parchment ground.
components.html:
¿Qué es la transustanciación?

Es la doctrina según la cual la sustancia del pan y del vino se convierte en la sustancia del cuerpo y la sangre de Cristo.
components.css: .ds-prose-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #DAD3C6; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.75rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,24,20,0.04), 0 8px 24px -12px rgba(26,24,20,0.18); transition: transform 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 0.4s ease; } .ds-prose-card:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); box-shadow: 0 2px 4px rgba(26,24,20,0.06), 0 20px 48px -20px rgba(26,24,20,0.28); } .ds-prose-card-icon { display: inline-flex; width: 3rem; height: 3rem; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border-radius: 50%; background: #EFEBE2; color: #C4922A; margin-bottom: 1rem; } .ds-prose-card-title { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.2rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1A1814; margin: 0 0 0.6rem; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-prose-card-body { font-family: 'Spectral', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #6B6560; line-height: 1.65; margin: 0; } .ds-prose-card-body strong { color: #1A1814; font-weight: 600; }

components.name: Debate Face — Urbina
components.description: Displays a debater's position in the Conflictos section. Always appears paired with its Pacheco counterpart.
components.html:
Dr. Dante Urbina

Apela a un consenso moral: basta un número suficiente de citas convergentes para inferir legítimamente que la doctrina ya se profesaba.
components.css: .ds-cara { padding: 1rem 1.75rem; border-radius: 4px; border-top: 3px solid; } .ds-cara-urbina { background: #E8EFF8; border-top-color: #1B4F8A; } .ds-cara-quien { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.83rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 500; color: #1B4F8A; margin: 0 0 0.4rem; } .ds-cara-texto { font-family: 'Spectral', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #1A1814; line-height: 1.55; margin: 0; }

components.name: Debate Face — Pacheco
components.description: Displays the Evangelical challenge position. Structurally identical to the Urbina face; crimson-coded.
components.html:
Sr. Edgar Pacheco

Esos mismos términos pueden denotar un cambio sacramental o de uso, no necesariamente un cambio de sustancia en sentido aristotélico.

components.name: Conclusion Item
components.description: <details>/<summary> accordion. Gospel Gold throughout. Opens with a lift shadow; chevron rotates 180°.
components.html:
1Los Padres usaron términos de cambio real

Metabolé, conversio, transformatio: estos vocablos implican un cambio en la cosa misma, no solo en su uso o en la percepción del fiel.
components.css: .ds-conclusion { background: linear-gradient(160deg, #ffffff, rgba(196,146,42,0.05)); border: 1px solid #DAD3C6; border-left: 4px solid #C4922A; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,24,20,0.04), 0 8px 24px -12px rgba(26,24,20,0.18); overflow: hidden; transition: box-shadow 0.3s ease; } .ds-conclusion[open] { box-shadow: 0 2px 4px rgba(26,24,20,0.06), 0 20px 48px -20px rgba(26,24,20,0.28); } .ds-conclusion-summary { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 1rem; padding: 1.75rem; cursor: pointer; list-style: none; } .ds-conclusion-summary::-webkit-details-marker { display: none; } .ds-conclusion-summary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #C4922A; outline-offset: -2px; } .ds-conclusion-n { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 1.44rem; color: #C4922A; flex-shrink: 0; line-height: 1; } .ds-conclusion-title { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.2rem; color: #163F6E; flex: 1; line-height: 1.25; } .ds-conclusion-chevron { flex-shrink: 0; color: #C4922A; transition: transform 0.3s ease; } .ds-conclusion[open] .ds-conclusion-chevron { transform: rotate(180deg); } .ds-conclusion-body { padding: 0 1.75rem 1.75rem; border-top: 1px solid #DAD3C6; padding-top: 1rem; } .ds-conclusion-body p { font-family: 'Spectral', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #1A1814; line-height: 1.7; margin: 0; }

components.name: Navigation
components.description: Fixed top bar. Ancient Parchment at 82% opacity with backdrop blur — the single authorized glassmorphism instance.
components.html:
✛Transustanciación & los Padres
ContextoArgumentosObjecionesVideos
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 0 2rem; height: 4.25rem; background: rgba(247,245,240,0.82); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border-bottom: 1px solid #DAD3C6; } .ds-nav-brand { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.6rem; text-decoration: none; color: #1A1814; } .ds-nav-mark { color: #C4922A; font-size: 1.1rem; } .ds-nav-title { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-nav-links { display: flex; gap: 1.5rem; } .ds-nav-links a { text-decoration: none; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.83rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #6B6560; padding-bottom: 0.25rem; position: relative; transition: color 0.2s ease; } .ds-nav-links a::after { content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0; bottom: 0; width: 100%; height: 1px; background: #C4922A; transform: scaleX(0); transform-origin: left; transition: transform 0.25s ease; } .ds-nav-links a:hover { color: #1A1814; } .ds-nav-links a:hover::after { transform: scaleX(1); }

components.name: Duelo — Signature
components.description: Hero centerpiece: three-column card presenting both debaters face to face. The visual thesis statement of the site.
components.html:
Postura católica

Dr. Dante Urbina

Teólogo y filósofo católico, especialista en patrística y apologética eucarística

vs
Postura evangélica

Sr. Edgar Pacheco

Teólogo evangélico reformado, crítico de la transustanciación
components.css: .ds-duelo { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr auto 1fr; align-items: stretch; border: 1px solid #DAD3C6; border-radius: 10px; overflow: hidden; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.4); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(26,24,20,0.04), 0 8px 24px -12px rgba(26,24,20,0.18); max-width: 46rem; } .ds-duelo-lado { padding: 1.75rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; align-items: center; text-align: center; } .ds-duelo-urbina { background: #E8EFF8; } .ds-duelo-pacheco { background: #F5EAEA; } .ds-duelo-bando { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 500; } .ds-duelo-urbina .ds-duelo-bando { color: #1B4F8A; } .ds-duelo-pacheco .ds-duelo-bando { color: #7A2E2E; } .ds-duelo-nombre { font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.2rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1A1814; margin: 0; } .ds-duelo-postura { font-family: 'Spectral', Georgia, serif; font-size: 0.83rem; color: #6B6560; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; } .ds-duelo-vs { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: 'Fraunces', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 1.2rem; color: #C4922A; background: #F7F5F0; padding: 0 1rem; border-left: 1px solid #DAD3C6; border-right: 1px solid #DAD3C6; }

narrative.overview:
The design is a courtroom, not a chapel. Two debaters — one Catholic, one Evangelical — are cross-examined by two thousand years of documented testimony. Every typographic and color decision carries rhetorical weight: what is large asserts; what is restrained defers; what is gold concludes. The interface is itself an argument.

Ancient Parchment (#F7F5F0) sets the ground: a warm, slightly textural page that reads as physical, not digital. On this ground, two competing palettes face each other — Ecclesial Navy for the Catholic position, Argument Crimson for the Evangelical challenge — organized by a single theological color law: blue argues forward, crimson argues back, and neither carries the final word. That belongs to Gospel Gold (#C4922A), which appears only at threshold moments: cited sources, stated conclusions, active selections. Its rarity is the argument.

Typography is the primary carrier. Fraunces handles display and structure; Spectral carries the body text; IBM Plex Mono voices the scholarly apparatus. This three-voice system encodes three editorial registers: the claim, the evidence, and the footnote. The page IS the design.
keyCharacteristics: Two-color dialectic (Navy vs. Crimson) that visually encodes the debate at every interactive level
Gospel Gold reserved for threshold moments — its rarity is rhetorical
Ancient Parchment ground treated as a manuscript page, not a software screen
Fraunces italic carries theological weight; Fraunces upright carries structural frame
IBM Plex Mono voices the scholarly apparatus: citations, labels, kickers, cross-references
Elevation responds to attention: surfaces lift only when the reader engages them
Pure HTML+CSS interactivity: :target for panels, <details> for collapsibles

rules.name: The Doctrinal Color Rule
rules.body: Ecclesial Navy belongs to the Catholic position; Argument Crimson belongs to the Evangelical challenge. Never invert these roles. Crimson must never signal correctness, approval, or primary action. Navy must never signal objection, challenge, or opposition.

rules.name: The Gospel Gold Rule
rules.body: Gold appears only at threshold moments: a citation referenced, a conclusion reached, an argument selected. Its share of any given screen must stay below 10%. More than that turns accent into noise.

rules.name: The Fraunces Italic Rule
rules.body: Fraunces italic carries the theological claim. Fraunces upright carries the structural container. If a word needs to feel like it means something, it goes italic. If it needs to feel like it organizes something, it goes upright.

rules.name: The Mono Apparatus Rule
rules.body: IBM Plex Mono speaks in the scholarly third person. It never leads; it annotates. Use it for: kickers, debater labels, source links, date/meta fields, panel close buttons, navigation links. Never for paragraph text.

rules.name: The Reader's Focus Rule
rules.body: The :target detail panel is always the highest-elevation surface visible on screen. Nothing else should match or exceed its shadow weight. Two competing Lift-shadow surfaces break this hierarchy.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Sections at the page level are flat. Only interactive leaves — tiles, cards, thumbnails — carry ambient shadows. Section backgrounds are tonal, not shadowed.

dos: Do color-code every component by debater: Ecclesial Navy for the Catholic position, Argument Crimson for the Evangelical challenge — without exception.
Do reserve Gospel Gold for threshold moments: cited sources, conclusions, active :target tiles, and the hero thesis statement.
Do present the Pacheco (Evangelical) arguments with identical structural dignity to the Urbina arguments — same tile pattern, same panel pattern, same typographic hierarchy.
Do use IBM Plex Mono exclusively for the scholarly apparatus: kickers, labels, source citations, panel sub-headings, navigation links. Never for paragraph text.
Do cap body copy at 65–75ch using max-width: var(--container-narrow) on all prose containers.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion: every transform and animation must have a reduced-motion alternative.
donts: Don't use stock-photo crosses, clip-art Catholic iconography, or gold filigree ornamental borders.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text combined with a gradient). Use a single solid color.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively. The navigation's backdrop-filter: blur(12px) is the single authorized instance.
Don't apply Argument Crimson to affirmative, success, or primary-action UI states.
Don't use the hero-metric template (large number, small label, gradient accent, stat row).
Don't extend the border-left: 4px solid stripe pattern to new components.
Don't build new card grids with identical structure (same icon + heading + text repeated).
Don't use Argument Crimson Deep as typographic emphasis outside Pacheco-coded panels.
